message:
feat(collect): add user-intent/context features (cfon.6)

Implement user-intent features per Plan §3.2 and §8 "context priors" that help
avoid false kills by detecting when a process is part of an active workflow.

New module: crates/pt-core/src/collect/user_intent.rs (1300+ lines)

Features:
- 10 signal types: active_tty, recent_tty_activity, tmux_session, screen_session,
  ssh_session, recent_shell_activity, active_repo_context, editor_focus,
  foreground_job, active_session_context
- Normalized user_intent_score (0.0-1.0) with configurable scoring methods
  (MaxWeight, WeightedAverage, Probabilistic)
- Structured evidence with per-signal weights and metadata
- Privacy-safe hashing (SHA-256 truncated to 16 hex chars)
- Policy-gated feature collection with opt-in for invasive signals
- Comprehensive provenance tracking for reproducibility

Privacy guarantees:
- All sensitive data (paths, IPs, session info) hashed by default
- Session multiplexer, SSH, and editor signals are opt-in
- Skipped signals explicitly tracked in privacy_mode.skipped_signals
- No raw file contents or sensitive paths in output

Test coverage: 29 unit tests covering deterministic fixtures, privacy validation,
serialization roundtrips, and scoring method correctness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
